Program Overview
Accredited & aligned to the
NHA (CET) & AMCA national standards

Read the heart's rhythm

As an EKG technician, you'll work alongside physicians, nurses and cardiac specialists in hospitals, clinics and cardiology offices. Our online EKG technician program teaches the skills that matter on the job.

You'll learn cardiac anatomy, the heart's electrical conduction system, EKG equipment and lead placement, and how to record and interpret rhythms — plus telemetry monitoring and quality patient care.

After finishing, you'll be ready to take the NHA Certified EKG Technician (CET) exam and/or the AMCA exam — nationally recognized credentials that open more job options and room to advance.
